question.text: What is the current annual interest rate payable on Sovereign Gold Bonds (SGBs)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SGBs bear an interest rate of currently 2.50% per annum on the initial investment amount, paid semi-annually. The bonds have an 8-year tenor, are denominated in grams of gold (minimum 1 gram), and can be traded on stock exchanges if held in demat form. **The Trap:** The maturity value of SGBs depends on the prevailing gold price, not just the coupon. Students may focus only on the interest component and forget the gold price exposure. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series V-A covers SGBs as an alternative investment product. Series XII covers listing and trading of SGBs. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** SGB = gold price exposure + 2.50% annual coupon. Win-win: if gold rises, you get capital appreciation plus guaranteed interest. No storage cost, no purity risk. 8-year tenor with exit option after 5th year.
